trade.summary: Bilateral trade covers Indonesian exports of palm oil, textiles, rubber, and furniture to Italy, while Italy exports machinery, industrial equipment, chemicals, and fashion goods. Italy is a key destination for Indonesian handicrafts and wood products in Europe.

diplomatic.summary: Italy and Indonesia maintain full diplomatic relations established in 1949. Interactions occur primarily through EU-ASEAN channels and G20. Italy has expressed interest in the Global South dialogue facilitated during Indonesia's G20 presidency in 2022.

military.summary: Limited defence cooperation. Some Italian naval vessels have conducted port calls in Indonesia. Leonardo (Italy) has discussed defence equipment with Indonesian armed forces.

financial.summary: Italian companies have invested modestly in Indonesian manufacturing and consumer goods sectors. ENI has historical energy sector interest in Indonesia. FDI flows are small relative to the size of both economies.

energy.summary: ENI has maintained upstream oil and gas operations in Indonesia. Limited LNG trade. Renewable energy dialogue is nascent.

scientific.summary: Academic exchange programmes exist between Italian and Indonesian universities. Collaboration in archaeology (Borobudur restoration expertise) and cultural heritage preservation is noteworthy.
